trivia_bullets.text: Duskmourn's plane is an infinite haunted mansion ruled by Valgavoth. Human Survivors are a named creature-type subgroup in DSK reflecting survival-horror genre tropes — characters making selfish or desperate choices to stay alive.
sources: Wizards of the Coast: Duskmourn mechanics article

trivia_bullets.text: "I know you'd want me to live, bestie!" — spoken by the depicted figure as she sacrifices a companion. The callousness is the mechanic: the card rewards sacrifice with life drain.

EDHREC rank 12,283 as of 2026-05-19. EDHREC set review describes it as 'a slightly downgraded Mayhem Devil' fitting sacrifice, Treasure, and enchantment-graveyard commander shells; can gain life off Treasure sacrifices in ways Mayhem Devil cannot.

The drain trigger fires when you sacrifice Popular Egotist itself, and fires individually per permanent when multiple permanents are sacrificed at once. The indestructible activation costs a sacrifice first — the drain cushions the cost.
Artist Julia Metzger; first printing with no reprint history.

related_cards.name: Mayhem Devil
related_cards.note: Direct design comparison cited by EDHREC — sacrifice-drain precedent, broader reach, no self-protection

related_cards.name: Arabella, Abandoned Doll
related_cards.note: Fellow DSK sacrifice payoff in the same horror-survival aesthetic

related_cards.name: Immersturm Predator
related_cards.note: Precedent for indestructible-via-sacrifice on a black creature
